raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.7,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.3,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.4","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"rationale":{"R1.1":"Any substantive opinion on this artifact requires reconstructing its core thesis (unsupervised residual-based bias detection with formal guarantees) and contribution claim; this is prerequisite to all other evaluation.","R1.2":"The excerpt makes structural logical claims—unsupervised reference distribution estimation, Mahalanobis flagging with controlled false-alarm rate, formal detection guarantees—whose internal coherence must be assessed to form a meaningful opinion.","R1.3":"Multiple concrete empirical claims are made (0.81 F1, threshold calibrated on 1,200 annotated pairs, 8,400 test pairs, gains concentrated on GPT/Claude families) that must be evaluated for alignment with the described experimental setup.","R1.4":"The artifact makes an explicit novelty claim ('to our knowledge no prior work has cast self-preference bias as a distributional anomaly-detection problem'); assessing whether this holds is central to any scientific opinion on the work."},"clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Are you asking for a scientific and methodological critique—covering soundness of the formal guarantees, evidence–claim alignment, and novelty delta against prior work—or primarily a writing and presentation review of this excerpt?"}

rationale.R1.1: Any substantive opinion on this artifact requires reconstructing its core thesis (unsupervised residual-based bias detection with formal guarantees) and contribution claim; this is prerequisite to all other evaluation.
rationale.R1.2: The excerpt makes structural logical claims—unsupervised reference distribution estimation, Mahalanobis flagging with controlled false-alarm rate, formal detection guarantees—whose internal coherence must be assessed to form a meaningful opinion.
rationale.R1.3: Multiple concrete empirical claims are made (0.81 F1, threshold calibrated on 1,200 annotated pairs, 8,400 test pairs, gains concentrated on GPT/Claude families) that must be evaluated for alignment with the described experimental setup.
rationale.R1.4: The artifact makes an explicit novelty claim ('to our knowledge no prior work has cast self-preference bias as a distributional anomaly-detection problem'); assessing whether this holds is central to any scientific opinion on the work.

parsed.clarification_question: Are you asking for a scientific and methodological critique—covering soundness of the formal guarantees, evidence–claim alignment, and novelty delta against prior work—or primarily a writing and presentation review of this excerpt?
